title: malus pumila
intro: Malus pumila, also known as the common apple or European crab apple, is a deciduous tree or shrub species in the rose family (Rosaceae). The bark and leaves of Malus pumila have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to their antimicrobial and antiparasitic properties. The health benefits of Malus pumila include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. Malus pumila can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
